text: I cannot tell you the level of stress that just like came out of my body the minute that I saw that.
I felt like, oh my gosh, I don't have to reply to every slack anymore.
It's kind of sparked like another level that I haven't felt since I first used cursor.
If I were to chat with my previous colleagues about console, they would just say sign me up.
When do I start? You know, where do I start?
I'm Danny. I lead IT over at cursor. I've been at cursor for about nine months.
When I first started, there was about 80 of us at the company total. Now we're close to over 600.
I'm Johnny, staff IT systems engineer for cursor.
My name is John. I'm an IT operations engineer here at cursor and I started about three months ago.
There's a lot of unique things that get requested to make some internal app work.
We've kind of become that central hub again.
We need far more engineers than we need people that are just going to respond to tickets.
So it's really this culture of building versus responding.
My first experience was I knew that there was no way that Danny was doing this all by himself as much as I love him.
I wanted to know what the secret sauce was.
Console fit in well with the vision that I had for cursor. How do I build this out when I add people?
Is this a tool that someone can come in, they actually look at it and go, oh wow, this really makes sense.
And it was console. With console, there is no learning curve. You define point A, two point B and console bridges that gap.
We had set it up in a way where we put some knowledge based articles and then it was kind of scanning the IT help channel to kind of learn.
And then there was a morning I remember I woke up and I immediately see this slack.
Someone's asking for access to something. I cannot tell you the level of stress that just like came out of my body the minute that I saw that.
I felt like, oh my gosh, I don't have to reply to every slack anymore.
I'm strictly support. I don't know code.
I made a playbook where I had console reach out to the employee to see like something that I shipped out, work.
And I'm like, did I really just do that?
I wanted to edit one of the existing playbooks that I have.
You know, it would have been one of those things where like if I was in the office, I would talk through it with John or Danny and see what they think.
And then I realized I could just do that directly with console. And then as soon as that clicked, it just kind of went crazy from there.
The fact that I can talk to console and say, hey, this is what I want to do to be able to start from that point and get to the end result without knowing how to code.
It does make me feel like I now have a seat at the table.
We have so many different offices that, you know, they get constant requests and so we want to help them automate.
Every time they brought something up, I would just look at Johnny and be like console, console, console.
To the point that people were like, what is this magic that you speak of?
I never thought that you could have a creative mindset in the IT world.
Console is the number one tool that I'm the most excited about in my IT stack currently.
It's kind of sparked like another level that I haven't felt since I first used cursor.
Honestly, it feels like an extension of the cursor IT team and more, dare I say family.
Thank you.
